title: hsqc-tocsy_metab.nan
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"50qeo","text":"This is a protocol for running the Bruker pulse program \"hsqcdietgpsisp.2\".","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9u1rj","text":"This protocol intends to provide concise instructions to carry out the experiment. For more comprehensive information, see Bruker's documentation \"Basic NMR Experiments\" by clicking ? →  Manuals (docs) on the menu bar on TopSpin. See also \"Pulse Program Catalogue. 1D/2D\" for the details about the pulse program used in this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3vmkh","text":"This protocol assumes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a4u94","text":"Your sample is loaded, locked, tuned for both proton and carbon channels, and shimmed in the magnet","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"an93o","text":"The calibrated 90° pulse value for proton (i.e., P1) for the sample has been collected","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: On the menu bar on TopSpin, click on Start → Create Dataset (This protocol uses TopSpin 3.6.4, and the interface may look different on other TopSpin versions.) You can also use the new command in the command line to do this step.
When Lock, Tune, Shim are complete proceed to the specific protocol for your desired pulseprogram(s).
Click on Acquire → Gain in the menu bar to automatically set the receiver gain. You can also use the rga command in the command line.
Go to the " USE DEFAULT " tab below to proceed with the default optimized parameters.

Load the calibrated P1 using the following command in the command line. getprosol 1H [calibrated P1 value] [power level for P1] (e.g., getprosol 1H 10.01 -7.45) [power level for P1] varies between spectrometers. Never use a wrong [power level for P1] .
After the run, click on Process → Proc. Spectrum in the menu bar to execute an automated processing macro.
If you want to modify parameters to improve your spectrum, go to step #2 and move to the step case " MODIFY PAR ".
Change parameters as needed. Typically, the main parameter to adjust is NS: Abbreviation Parameter Note 1 NS Number of scans To increase the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N). S/N increases as √NS

Other potential parameters to adjust: Abbreviation Parameter Note 1 TD Size of FID To optimize the digital resolution 2 SW Spectral width To minimize unnecessary regions 3 AQ Acquisition time Changing TD or SW will also change AQ. Never exceed 0.3 s. 0.1–0.2 s is sufficient 4 O1P Transmitter frequency offset To adjust the center of a spectrum 5 DS Number of dummy scans To allow the system to achieve a steady state. At least ≥16 6 D9 TOCSY mixing time To adjust TOCSY mixing time Maintaining AQ of <0.3 s is especially important since this experiment uses carbon decoupling during acquisition DS of ≥16 is also important for HSQC-TOCSY since carbon decoupling is on during acquisition and can heat the probe and sample
Other potential parameter for the NUS parameter sets: Abbreviation Parameter Note 1 NusAMOUNT Amount of sparse sampling for NUS To adjust the number of FIDs to collect

A new window opens. On the right top bar, select Source = /opt/NAN_METAB/par In the list, select the one you want to use: For serum and plasma samples: HSQC-TOCSY_br600_serum.par : Parameter set using an acquisition mode "traditional planes" HSQC-TOCSY_NUS_br600_serum.par : Parameter set using an acquisition mode "non-uniform sampling (NUS)". Higher resolution on the indirect dimension For urine samples: HSQC-TOCSY_br600_urine.par : Parameter set using an acquisition mode "traditional planes" HSQC-TOCSY_NUS_br600_urine.par : Parameter set using an acquisition mode "non-uniform sampling (NUS)". Higher resolution on the indirect dimension Parameter set names in the list vary between spectrometers (e.g., HSQC-TOCSY_br800_serum.par). Click OK
Select Read parameterset Click the button Select

Enter NAME : Name of a set of datasets (e.g., human_serum_study1). Use a single string EXPNO : Dataset number. Use a positive integer Select Directory : Your directory Your new dataset will be stored in Directory/NAME/EXPNO .
Click Go in the menu bar to acquire a spectrum. You can also use the zg command in the command line.

Select the AcquPars tab to show a parameter set. There are two columns: F2 is for the direct dimension, and F1 is for the indirect (13C) dimension. You can also use the ased command in the command line.
Click the icon _∏_ to see a complete parameter set. You can also use the eda command in the command line.

Acquire a spectrum. If more optimization is needed, go to step #3.3 . You can check the experiment time after any change by using the expt command After collecting >16 FIDs, you can process the data while the experiment is still running. As an example, for S/N evaluation, if you see only very noisy signals after collecting >16 FIDs, you can stop the experiment by using the stop command and then increase NS
creator: NAN-KB UGA
